programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The evolution from wooden ships to metal ones in the second half of the 19th century is examined.

programme.desc: The history of the B-1 Lancer reveals how the Cold War bomber evolved to become one of Americas most effective military aircraft.

programme.desc: A countdown of the most influential and impactful military planes of the Cold War, from America's B-2 to the Soviet's MiG-15.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The inside story of the F-86 Sabre, one of America's first fighter jets, and its epic battles against the Soviet MiG jet during the Korean War and beyond.

programme.desc: A look at the top U.S. military aircraft in America's modern arsenal, including the F-117 Nighthawk stealth fighter, the hybrid V-22 Osprey, and more.

programme.desc: The North American F-100 Super Saber, also known as the Hun, becomes a supersonic powerhouse after initial difficulties; it was used primarily in the Vietnam War.

programme.desc: A look at how the C-47 evolved from passenger plane to a transport aircraft and flying hospital in WWII and years later as a gunship in Vietnam.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: Stories of fighter aircraft and deadly combat, including dogfights with the P-38 Lightning and Messerschmitts over Nazi Germany, told through rarely seen battle footage and first-hand accounts.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When a USAF stealth bomber crashes after takeoff, investigators search for a reason.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When a modified WWII fighter plane crashes into the crowd at a Nevada air show, investigators struggle to find the cause.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A quick trip from Washington D.C. to Ohio in 1994 turns deadly when pilots of a twin-engine turboprop attempt a risky landing.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: The two-man crew of a UPS cargo jet must attempt an emergency landing when a fire in the cargo hold fills their cockpit with smoke.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Investigators try to find the cause of two similar air crashes that happened nearly 12 years apart.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Polish Air Force Flight 101, carrying the President of Poland Lech Kaczynski, his wife Maria Kaczynska and other top officials, crashes in a wooded area while on final approach to Smolensk North Airport in heavy fog and low visibility.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A billion-dollar Stealth Bomber crashes at a U.S. Air Force base-the costliest aviation disaster ever; bad data misleads a charter flight into a Portuguese mountain and conflicting alarms send a cutting-edge jetliner plunging into the Atlantic.

rating.system: Dirección General de Radio, Televisión y Cinematografía
Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: With hidden flaws buried in their navigation systems, three planes suffer catastrophic computer errors that force investigators to dig deep for clues.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Moments before Pacific Southwest Flight 1771 crashes on a cattle ranch in California, gunshots are heard in the cabin.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When a twin-engine jet careens off the runway in Kentucky, investigators must determine why the plane failed to take off.

programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: Experts examine how the Chernobyl nuclear disaster and the Bhopal gas tragedy happened; they also explore the consequences and how they could have been avoided.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: In 1991, a Boeing 737 operating as United Airlines Flight 585 suddenly rolls into a dive and crashes within eight seconds, killing all 25 people on board.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When Germanwings Flight 9525 crashes in the French Alps, investigators discover shocking truths about the co-pilot's mental health.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: On 27 November 2008, an aircraft on a post-maintenance test flight operating as XL Airways Germany Flight 888T stalls and crashes into the Mediterranean Sea off the coast of France, killing all seven people on board.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: In 1999, EgyptAir Flight 990 crashes into the Atlantic Ocean while flying from New York City to Cairo, killing all 217 people on board; the cause of the crash is disputed.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Northwest Airlines Flight 255 crashes shortly after take-off in Detroit in 1987; a fatal preflight error caused the second-deadliest plane crash in the U.S. history.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Three crews experience the same devastating event; with radically different outcomes when an engine falls off their commercial airplane.

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: The Wright Brothers gave the state its slogan "First in Flight"; civil rights leaders in Greensboro; NASCAR.

programme.desc: A cross-country flight across America reveals the nation's most iconic industries, the people behind them and the stories of how they came to be.

programme.desc: A flight over North and South Dakota reveals the shared history and the vast differences of these sibling states.
